Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper says the PsbE N-terminal domain is soluble and exposed to the stromal side of the thylakoids, supporting thylakoid membrane localization with stromal-exposed N-terminus.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state PsbE-N had much lower GFP accumulation than PetB-N, PetD-N and PGK-N, while PsbE-C caused only moderately lower accumulation than some other C-termini.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are directly reflected in the Results: PsbE N-terminus was the main reporter scaffold, penultimate-residue substitutions caused strong stability differences, and PsbE-N itself gave relatively low accumulation compared with several other N-termini.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods were all used in the study: transplastomic expression, codon exchange, GFP reporter assays, Western/RNA blots, polysome analysis, and MS for the Glu variant derived from the PsbE-GFP scaffold.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Figure 1 and methods describe the gfp reporter under Prrn with a 5' UTR containing an rbcL-derived Shine-Dalgarno sequence.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper identifies PsbE as a PSII component and uses only its terminal peptide sequences as reporter fusions rather than testing PsbE metabolic function.

corrections.reason: No evidence that the source PsbE sequence specifically came from Petit Havana variety.
